issue.title: GitClaw channel poll vote E2E 20260602T101858Z
issue.body:
@gitclaw /channels poll e2e-slack-route,e2e-telegram-route --poll-id poll-vote-20260602T101858Z --message-id poll-vote-invite-20260602T101858Z

Question: Which channel poll vote path should ship for NOECHO_CHANNEL_POLL_VOTE_QUESTION_20260602T101858Z?
Options:
- Ship the poll vote path
- Keep the poll invite only

GitClaw created or reused a GitHub poll issue, then queued one provider-facing poll invitation per reviewed route. The poll issue contains the human-readable question and options; this source receipt keeps route names, poll IDs, question text, option text, thread IDs, message IDs, and outbound bodies out of band.

### Destinations
- destination=`01` target_issue=`#706` outbound_comment_id=`4601415046` target_issue_created=`true` duplicate_suppressed=`false` route_sha256_12=`ff6bd0e4b42c` channel=`slack` thread_id_sha256_12=`bd4310812397` message_id_sha256_12=`70ba1525b733` body_sha256_12=`e9434e8951fd`
- destination=`02` target_issue=`#707` outbound_comment_id=`4601415251` target_issue_created=`true` duplicate_suppressed=`false` route_sha256_12=`325d7a85c564` channel=`telegram` thread_id_sha256_12=`f8c2b9b06078` message_id_sha256_12=`70ba1525b733` body_sha256_12=`e9434e8951fd`

### Follow-Up Delivery
- provider gateways read pending poll invites with `gitclaw channel-outbox --channel <provider> --account-id <account> --issue-number <issue> --out <file>`
- provider gateways record sent poll invites with `gitclaw channel-delivery --channel <provider> --account-id <account> --issue-number <issue> --comment-id <comment> --external-message-id <message>`
- duplicate poll invites are suppressed independently for each route by `channel + message_id`
